message:
Fix test script to properly set up git tracking branches

- Create main branch explicitly before first commit
- Use git push -u to set upstream tracking in one command

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -31,16 +31,16 @@ {"id":"bd-1204","title":"Implement get_install_dir() and get_shell_config()","description":"**Determine installation directory and shell config** ## What Functions to determine where to install and which shell config to modify. ## Why Install location and PATH handling varies by system and user preference. ## get_install_dir() ```bash get_install_dir() { if [[ -n "${DEST:-}" ]]; then echo "$DEST" elif [[ "${RU_SYSTEM:-}" == "1" ]]; then echo "/usr/local/bin" else echo "${HOME}/.local/bin" fi } ``` ## get_shell_config() ```bash get_shell_config() { local shell shell=$(basename "$SHELL") case "$shell" in zsh) echo "${HOME}/.zshrc" ;; bash) if [[ -f "${HOME}/.bashrc" ]]; then echo "${HOME}/.bashrc" else echo "${HOME}/.bash_profile" fi ;; *) echo "${HOME}/.profile" ;; esac } ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - Respects DEST override - Detects shell correctly - Uses appropriate config file","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:23:28.464814474-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:23:28.464814474-05:00","close_reason":"All functions implemented in install.sh","labels":["installer"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1204","depends_on_id":"bd-1201","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.57179746-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"bd-1205","title":"Implement add_to_path()","description":"**Add installation directory to PATH** ## What Offer to add the install directory to PATH if not already present. ## Why Users need ru in their PATH. We help with this but don't force it. ## Implementation ```bash add_to_path() { local install_dir="$1" local shell_config shell_config=$(get_shell_config) # Check if already in PATH if [[ ":$PATH:" == *":$install_dir:"* ]]; then return 0 # Already there fi # Check if config already exports it if grep -q "export PATH=.*$install_dir" "$shell_config" 2>/dev/null; then echo "PATH export already in $shell_config" >&2 return 0 fi echo "" >&2 echo "$install_dir is not in your PATH." >&2 echo "Add this to $shell_config:" >&2 echo " export PATH=\"$install_dir:\$PATH\"" >&2 echo "" >&2 # Non-interactive: just inform if [[ ! -t 0 ]]; then return 0 fi read -p "Add it now? [y/N] " response if [[ "${response,,}" == "y" ]]; then echo "" >> "$shell_config" echo "# Added by ru installer" >> "$shell_config" echo "export PATH=\"$install_dir:\$PATH\"" >> "$shell_config" echo "Added to $shell_config. Restart your shell or run: source $shell_config" >&2 fi } ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - Detects if already in PATH - Prompts interactively - Just informs in non-interactive mode - Idempotent (doesn't add twice)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:23:28.466383299-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:23:28.466383299-05:00","close_reason":"All functions implemented in install.sh","labels":["installer"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1205","depends_on_id":"bd-1204","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.60493115-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"bd-1206","title":"Implement install.sh main()","description":"**Complete installation flow** ## What The main function that orchestrates the entire installation. ## Why Brings all pieces together for a complete, working installer. ## Flow 1. Print banner 2. Determine version (RU_VERSION or latest) 3. Check for RU_UNSAFE_MAIN (warn loudly if set) 4. Determine install directory 5. Check if curl/wget available 6. Download and verify 7. Handle PATH 8. Print success and next steps ## Implementation Skeleton ```bash main() { echo "=================================" >&2 echo " ru installer" >&2 echo "=================================" >&2 local version="${RU_VERSION:-}" local install_dir install_dir=$(get_install_dir) # Warning for unsafe mode if [[ "${RU_UNSAFE_MAIN:-}" == "1" ]]; then echo "WARNING: Installing from main branch (unverified)" >&2 download_from_main "$install_dir" else if [[ -z "$version" ]]; then version=$(get_latest_release) fi download_and_verify "$version" "$install_dir" fi add_to_path "$install_dir" echo "" >&2 echo "Installation complete!" >&2 echo "" >&2 echo "Next steps:" >&2 echo " 1. Run: ru init" >&2 echo " 2. Add repos: ru add owner/repo" >&2 echo " 3. Sync: ru sync" >&2 } main "$@" ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - Complete working installer - Secure by default - Clear output - Helpful next steps","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:23:28.468096506-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:23:28.468096506-05:00","close_reason":"All functions implemented in install.sh","labels":["installer"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1206","depends_on_id":"bd-1202","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.636366511-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-1206","depends_on_id":"bd-1203","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.667157086-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-1206","depends_on_id":"bd-1205","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.698424841-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} -{"id":"bd-13","title":"Phase 13: README & Documentation","description":"**EPIC: Comprehensive README Documentation** ## Goal Create thorough documentation in giil-style: beautiful, comprehensive, with examples for every use case. ## Rationale Documentation is marketing. A well-crafted README converts visitors into users. It should answer every question a potential user might have, with copy-pasteable examples. ## README Sections - Hero banner with badges - Primary use case (the 'why') - Quickstart (under 30 seconds to first value) - All commands with examples - Configuration reference - Automation/CI guide with exit codes - Troubleshooting - Architecture overview - License ## Examples Must Be Runnable Every code block should be something a user can actually copy and run. No pseudocode, no placeholders without explanation. ## Success Criteria - README covers all features - Quickstart actually works for new users - Examples are copy-paste ready","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["docs","readme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-13","depends_on_id":"bd-10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.511033657-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} -{"id":"bd-1301","title":"Create README.md header with badges","description":"**Create README header with project identity** ## What Create the README.md with hero section, badges, and brief description. ## Why First impression matters. The header should immediately communicate what the tool is and its quality. ## Badges to Include - CI status - Latest release version - License (MIT) - Shell (bash) ## Content ```markdown

ru

Repo Updater

A beautiful, automation-friendly CLI for synchronizing GitHub repositories

``` ## Acceptance Criteria - Badges display correctly - Centered, professional look - Brief, accurate description","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1301","depends_on_id":"bd-13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.730074625-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} -{"id":"bd-1302","title":"Write quickstart and commands sections","description":"**Document quickstart and all commands** ## What Write the quickstart guide and complete command reference. ## Why Users need to get started quickly and find reference documentation easily. ## Quickstart Section ```markdown ## Quickstart # Install curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/repo_updater/main/install.sh | bash # Initialize ru init # Add repos ru add owner/repo1 ru add owner/repo2 --private # Sync everything ru sync ``` ## Commands Section - Table of all commands with descriptions - Each command with full options - Real examples for each ## Acceptance Criteria - 30-second quickstart works for new users - All commands documented - Examples are copy-paste ready","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1302","depends_on_id":"bd-1301","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.758079272-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-1302","depends_on_id":"bd-1001","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.787654137-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} -{"id":"bd-1303","title":"Write automation/CI section","description":"**Document CI/automation usage** ## What Explain how to use ru in CI/CD pipelines and scripts. ## Why Automation is a key use case. Users need to know exit codes and non-interactive mode. ## Content ```markdown ## Automation & CI ru is designed for non-interactive use: # In CI/scripts: ru sync --non-interactive --json # With environment auth: GH_TOKEN=xxx ru sync --non-interactive ### Exit Codes | Code | Meaning | |------|-------------------------------------------| | 0 | All repos synced successfully | | 1 | Some repos failed (network, auth, etc.) | | 2 | Conflicts exist (diverged, dirty, etc.) | | 3 | Dependency error (gh missing, no auth) | | 4 | Invalid arguments | ### GitHub Actions Example jobs: sync-repos: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4 - run: curl -fsSL .../install.sh | bash - run: ru sync --non-interactive env: GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GH_TOKEN }} ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - Exit codes documented - GH_TOKEN pattern explained - GitHub Actions example works","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1303","depends_on_id":"bd-1302","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.818615174-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-1303","depends_on_id":"bd-1105","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.849991924-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} -{"id":"bd-1304","title":"Write troubleshooting and architecture sections","description":"**Document troubleshooting and architecture** ## What Common issues and solutions, plus architecture overview for contributors. ## Why Users need help when things go wrong. Contributors need to understand the codebase. ## Troubleshooting - 'gh not found' - installation instructions - 'authentication required' - GH_TOKEN or gh auth login - 'diverged' - resolution commands - 'remote mismatch' - explanation and fix ## Architecture - Script organization diagram - Configuration flow - Error handling philosophy (not set -e) - Stream separation explanation ## Acceptance Criteria - Common issues covered - Clear resolution steps - Architecture understandable for contributors","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1304","depends_on_id":"bd-1302","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.880652474-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} -{"id":"bd-14","title":"Phase 14: CI/CD Workflows","description":"**EPIC: GitHub Actions CI/CD Workflows** ## Goal Implement CI workflows for quality assurance and release automation. ## Rationale Automated testing catches issues before they reach users. ShellCheck finds common shell scripting errors. Behavioral tests verify actual functionality. Release automation ensures consistent, secure distribution. ## CI Workflow (ci.yml) - ShellCheck on all .sh files - Bash syntax validation (bash -n) - Installation test on Ubuntu and macOS - URL parsing tests - Local git operation tests - JSON output validation - Version consistency check ## Release Workflow (release.yml) - Triggered on version tags (v*) - Creates GitHub Release - Generates SHA256 checksums - Uploads ru and install.sh as release assets ## Success Criteria - PRs are blocked on CI failure - Releases are automated and include checksums - Tests actually catch real bugs","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["ci","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-14","depends_on_id":"bd-1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.540476483-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-14","depends_on_id":"bd-7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.569867903-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-14","depends_on_id":"bd-8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.598982951-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} -{"id":"bd-1401","title":"Create .github/workflows/ci.yml","description":"**Create CI workflow for PRs and pushes** ## What GitHub Actions workflow for continuous integration. ## Why Automated testing catches bugs before they reach users. ## Jobs 1. **shellcheck**: Lint all shell scripts 2. **syntax-check**: Bash syntax validation 3. **install-test**: Test installer on Ubuntu and macOS 4. **behavioral-tests**: Run URL parsing and git operation tests 5. **version-consistency**: Verify VERSION file matches script ## Implementation ```yaml name: CI on: push: branches: [main] pull_request: branches: [main] jobs: shellcheck: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4 - uses: ludeeus/action-shellcheck@master with: severity: warning syntax-check: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4 - run: bash -n ru - run: bash -n install.sh # ... more jobs ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - All jobs run on PRs - ShellCheck catches issues - Tests actually test functionality","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["ci"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1401","depends_on_id":"bd-14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.910288124-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} -{"id":"bd-1402","title":"Create .github/workflows/release.yml","description":"**Create release workflow** ## What GitHub Actions workflow for automated releases. ## Why Automated releases ensure consistent, secure distribution. ## Trigger On push of version tags: v* ## Steps 1. Validate tag matches VERSION file 2. Create GitHub Release 3. Generate SHA256 checksums 4. Upload ru and install.sh as assets 5. Upload checksums ## Implementation ```yaml name: Release on: push: tags: - 'v*' jobs: release: runs-on: ubuntu-latest permissions: contents: write steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4 - name: Verify version consistency run: | TAG_VERSION="${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/v}" FILE_VERSION=$(cat VERSION) if [[ "$TAG_VERSION" != "$FILE_VERSION" ]]; then echo "Version mismatch: tag=$TAG_VERSION file=$FILE_VERSION" exit 1 fi - name: Generate checksums run: | sha256sum ru > ru.sha256 sha256sum install.sh > install.sh.sha256 - name: Create Release uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v1 with: files: | ru ru.sha256 install.sh install.sh.sha256 ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - Releases created on tag push - Checksums included - Version consistency enforced","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["ci","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1402","depends_on_id":"bd-1401","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.940855899-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} +{"id":"bd-13","title":"Phase 13: README & Documentation","description":"**EPIC: Comprehensive README Documentation** ## Goal Create thorough documentation in giil-style: beautiful, comprehensive, with examples for every use case. ## Rationale Documentation is marketing. Upload checksums ## Implementation ```yaml name: Release on: push: tags: - 'v*' jobs: release: runs-on: ubuntu-latest permissions: contents: write steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4 - name: Verify version consistency run: | TAG_VERSION="${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/v}" FILE_VERSION=$(cat VERSION) if [[ "$TAG_VERSION" != "$FILE_VERSION" ]]; then echo "Version mismatch: tag=$TAG_VERSION file=$FILE_VERSION" exit 1 fi - name: Generate checksums run: | sha256sum ru > ru.sha256 sha256sum install.sh > install.sh.sha256 - name: Create Release uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v1 with: files: | ru ru.sha256 install.sh install.sh.sha256 ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - Releases created on tag push - Checksums included - Version consistency enforced","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:41:44.65024076-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:41:44.65024076-05:00","close_reason":"Workflows already created in .github/workflows/","labels":["ci","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1402","depends_on_id":"bd-1401","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.940855899-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"bd-1403","title":"Create scripts/test_parsing.sh","description":"**Implement URL parsing tests** ## What Test script that validates URL parsing functions. ## Why Parsing is critical infrastructure. Bugs here break everything. ## Test Structure ```bash #!/usr/bin/env bash set -euo pipefail SOURCE_DIR="$(dirname "$0")/.." source "$SOURCE_DIR/ru" FAILED=0 assert_parses() { local url="$1" expected_host="$2" expected_owner="$3" expected_repo="$4" local host owner repo if parse_repo_url "$url" host owner repo; then if [[ "$host" != "$expected_host" || "$owner" != "$expected_owner" || "$repo" != "$expected_repo" ]]; then echo "FAIL: $url parsed to $host/$owner/$repo (expected $expected_host/$expected_owner/$expected_repo)" ((FAILED++)) else echo "PASS: $url" fi else echo "FAIL: $url failed to parse" ((FAILED++)) fi } # Test cases assert_parses "https://github.com/owner/repo" "github.com" "owner" "repo" assert_parses "https://github.com/owner/repo.git" "github.com" "owner" "repo" assert_parses "git@github.com:owner/repo.git" "github.com" "owner" "repo" assert_parses "owner/repo" "github.com" "owner" "repo" exit $FAILED ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - All URL formats tested - Clear pass/fail output - Returns non-zero on any failure","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:38:03.715742132-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:38:03.715742132-05:00","close_reason":"scripts/test_parsing.sh exists and all 8 tests pass","labels":["testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1403","depends_on_id":"bd-706","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.975042544-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} -{"id":"bd-1404","title":"Create scripts/test_local_git.sh","description":"**Implement local git operation tests** ## What Test script that validates git operations without network. ## Why Git operations must work correctly. @@ -122,11 +122,13 @@ init_repo_with_commit() {
     cd "$work_dir"
     git config user.email "test@test.com"
     git config user.name "Test User"
+    # Explicitly create main branch (cloning empty repo has no branch)
+    git checkout -b main 2>/dev/null || true
     echo "initial content" > file.txt
     git add file.txt
     git commit -m "Initial commit" >/dev/null 2>&1
-    git push origin HEAD:main >/dev/null 2>&1
-    git branch --set-upstream-to=origin/main main 2>/dev/null || true
+    # Use -u to set upstream tracking in one command
+    git push -u origin main >/dev/null 2>&1
     cd - >/dev/null
 }
 
@@ -218,7 +220,7 @@ test_status_behind() {
     # Create initial repo
     init_repo_with_commit "$remote" "$dev_dir"
 
-    # Clone to projects dir
+    # Clone to projects dir (repo now has content, so tracking works)
     git clone "$remote" "$work_dir" >/dev/null 2>&1
     cd "$work_dir" && git config user.email "test@test.com" && git config user.name "Test" && cd - >/dev/null
